disease.name: Tomato Early Blight
symptoms: Dark spots with concentric rings on older leaves
Yellowing leaf margins around spot spots
Premature leaf drop leading to foliage decay
causes: Fungal spores overwintering in plant crop debris
chemicalTreatment: Spray Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb fungicide immediately
organicTreatment: Spray Neem Oil extract (5ml per liter water) weekly
Apply copper bio-fungicide to soil base

pesticideDetails.dosage: 2 grams per liter of clean water
pesticideDetails.mixingMethod: Dissolve powder in clean water. Spray uniformly on under and upper leaf surfaces in morning.
pesticideDetails.precautions: Wear protective gloves and face mask. Wash hands after use. Avoid spraying during strong winds.
pesticideDetails.waitingPeriod: 7 days waiting period before harvesting crops

preventiveTips: Rotate tomato crops with non-solanaceous crops every 3 years
Drip irrigate at base of plants to avoid wetting foliage
Mulch soil to prevent rain splash infection

cropRecommend.name: Wheat (Rabi season)
cropRecommend.description: Excellent crop choice for loam soils during winter. MSP support guarantees sales.

cropRecommend.name: Chickpeas (Gram)
cropRecommend.description: Fixes nitrogen in the soil. Requires low irrigation and yields good market prices.

fertilizerPlan: Nitrogen is deficient. Apply 50 kg Urea per acre in split doses.
Phosphorus is moderate. Apply 30 kg DAP during sowing.
Potassium is optimal. No extra potash fertilizer required.
soilImprovementSuggestions: Incorporate 5 tons of Farm Yard Manure (FYM) to boost organic carbon.
Soil is slightly acidic. Apply 100 kg lime per acre to neutralize.

weatherAdvice: Clear skies with light winds. Ideal day for spraying pesticides and sowing seeds.
